title: Listening-Session Reconstruction

landmines.name: order-by-play-id
landmines.kills: Ordering events by play_id (ingestion order) instead of played_at scrambles session boundaries because the firehose is not chronological. See naive/1_order_by_play_id.sql.

landmines.name: gap-off-by-one-30min
landmines.kills: Using a >= 30-minute gap test instead of > 30 minutes splits a session at an exactly-30-minute gap, inflating the session count. See naive/2_gte_30_boundary.sql.

landmines.name: duplicate-events-not-deduped
landmines.kills: Sessionizing the raw firehose without deduping on (user_id, track_id, played_at) lets retry duplicates inflate session play counts. See naive/4_no_dedup.sql.

landmines.name: selfjoin-predecessor-tle
landmines.kills: Finding each event's predecessor with a correlated self-join is O(n^2) per listener and times out on the multi-million-row hidden fixture; a single LAG window pass is required. See naive/3_selfjoin_predecessor_tle.sql (correct output, TLE).

notes: Timestamp gap arithmetic is not portable across the four engines, so a per-engine reference is shipped (reference.sql = SQLite unixepoch; reference.postgres.sql = EXTRACT(EPOCH...); reference.duckdb.sql = date_diff; reference.mysql.sql = TIMESTAMPDIFF) -- all verified to agree on sqlite/duckdb/postgres. mssql omitted (needs DATEDIFF/SUBSTRING). Naives are written in the SQLite dialect matching reference.sql; each landmine's logic error is engine-independent. naive/1 needs play_id order to differ from played_at order (true at hidden scale; proven on a scramble fixture) and naive/4 needs a duplicate event (present at every scale); naive/2 is proven divergent on the worked-example fixture; naive/3 matches output and is the designated TLE naive-slow for section-6 calibration.
